task_completion.description: Verify the scenario directory was created
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command (like 'mkdir' or 'ls') or a file manager window showing the directory '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/Blind Pilotage Calibration' exists.
Look for the contents of 'environment.ini' in a text editor or terminal output showing the area set to 'Solent', 'VisibilityRange' set to 0.25, and 'StartTime' set to 10.0.

task_completion.description: Verify othership.ini contains at least one dummy vessel
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'othership.ini' showing at least one vessel defined (typically with parameters like type, start coordinates, or waypoints).

task_completion.description: Verify ownship.ini is configured with correct parameters and calculated longitude
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ownship.ini' contents showing latitude 50.6000, heading 000, speed 10.0, and a calculated starting longitude. The longitude should be approximately -0.9359 (or 0.9359W).

task_completion.description: Verify the briefing file contains the calculated longitude and PI range
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the file '/home/ga/Documents/pi_calibration_brief.txt' being viewed or printed, containing the calculated starting longitude (matching ownship.ini) and mentioning the 0.6 nm Parallel Index range.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration files were actually saved to disk
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for saved file indicators in the text editor (e.g., no unsaved asterisk next to the filename) or terminal commands like 'cat' verifying the contents are on disk.

integrity.description: Verify the scenario files use valid INI formatting
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the .ini files to ensure they use standard INI syntax (e.g., Key=Value pairs, optionally under [Section] headers) rather than unstructured text or JSON.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't use a placeholder for the longitude
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the briefing file contains an actual numerical value for the longitude that matches the one in ownship.ini, rather than a placeholder like '[Insert Longitude]'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Nab Tower is located at 50.6675°N. At this latitude, 1 minute of longitude equals approximately 0.6338 nautical miles (or 1 degree = 38.028 nm). To pass exactly 0.6 nm East of 0.9517° W, the starting longitude must be calculated: 0.6 nm / 38.028 nm/deg = 0.01577 degrees. 0.9517° W - 0.01577° = 0.9359° W (or -0.9359). Accept values close to -0.9359 or 0.9359W in ownship.ini and the briefing file.
